THE
HUB

4 concepts  ·  1 vision  ·  High-output excellence

◈ Poblenou, Barcelona

The Hub was born from the conviction that efficiency is not the enemy of pleasure. Each concept is engineered to deliver a precise, fast, and authentic culinary experience — in spaces where the rawness of steel and polished concrete finds balance with the warmth of light wood and stabilised moss walls.

The Hub · 01 / 04

THE HUB
PIZZA

High-temperature stone oven · Speed of execution · Fire-crafted, every time.

The Concept

A wide range of fully customisable pizzas built around a unique rectangular format — 20 cm wide, with a length chosen by the customer: from 30 cm up to 1.5 metres. Share it, split it, make it yours.

The Differentiator

A flexible, shareable product unlike any round-pizza competitor. The format adapts to solo diners, couples, and large groups — maximising average ticket and table experience.

20 cm
Fixed width
30 cm → 1.5 m
Your chosen length
∞
Topping combinations

© 2025 The Hub. All rights reserved.

The Hub · 02 / 04

THE HUB
SMASH

Gourmet burgers · High-output production · The perfect crust, every single time.

The Concept

Organic beef burgers in a wide variety of combinations, complemented by fries, salads, and side dishes — positioned firmly in the higher-quality segment. Every patty smashed to order on a hot chrome griddle.

The Differentiator

100% organic beef meets the smash technique's signature crust — bridging fast-food speed with premium-restaurant quality at a sustainable price point.

Organic Beef
Traceable sourcing
Smash Crust
Chrome griddle · High heat
Sides & Salads
Full meal offer

The Hub · 03 / 04

THE HUB
JAPAS

Precision · Freshness · Raw Station — Sushi & Japanese tapas, elevated.

The Concept

A small-format Japanese culinary offering combining speed with culinary richness. Gyozas, baozi, dumplings, fried wontons, tuna and salmon tartares, soups, and wok-based dishes — all crafted at the Raw Station.

The Differentiator

Japanese tapas are inherently social and shareable — driving higher average spend per table while matching the pace of a high-output operation.

Gyozas & Baozi
Steamed & pan-fried
Tartares & Raw

The Hub · 04 / 04

THE HUB
POKÉ

Health · Freshness · Full personalisation — Cold Station, built your way.

The Concept

A highly customisable bowl experience where customers define every element — size, base, proteins, ingredients, sauces and toppings. Aligned with current trends in healthy, flexible eating.

The Differentiator

Full personalisation means every customer is the chef. The Cold Station model keeps ingredients fresh and fast to assemble for both the health-conscious lunch crowd and the quality-seeking dinner guest.

Inside The Hub

LEARN
MORE

01 —

Location & Legal Strategy

Strategic choice · Poblenou, Barcelona

A neighbourhood blending tech-workers at lunch and high-end residents at dinner — engineered for two distinct clienteles and maximum seat utilisation across the full day.

The Hybrid Advantage

Unlike pure dark kitchens constrained by the 400m restriction (Colau/Collboni regulation), The Hub operates under a mixed restauration licence — enabling installation in dense urban zones where industrial kitchens are banned outright.

Legal Justification

The plan includes a fully compliant on-site consumption area (40+ seats) and a central bar. This hybridisation secures our position in the urban fabric and differentiates us from delivery-only competitors.

02 —

Layout & Operational Flow

The space is divided into high-performance zones, each engineered to eliminate cross-contamination and maximise throughput.

Zone 01
The Bar Magnet
Heart of the Hub

The circular central bar manages all beverages and guest reception, acts as a soft psychological barrier between production and the dining zone, and centralises billing for all 4 brands.

Zone 02
Receiving & Storage
Back of House

A single dedicated technical entrance for all suppliers, flowing directly into shared cold rooms. One entry point eliminates cross-traffic and enables strict traceability.

Zone 03
4 Production Cells
Independent Kitchens

Each concept occupies its own independent kitchen quadrant with dedicated air extraction. Zero cross-contamination by design.

Zone 04
Central Plonge
Centralised Dishwashing

Strategically positioned to collect crockery from both dining room and all production kitchens without disrupting service flow. 90-second rapid-cycle dishwasher.

03 —

Neurodesign & Well-being

Method · Marta Ribas · Cognitive Sciences

We apply cognitive science to improve both staff performance and guest experience. Every design decision — light, sound, spatial navigation — is evidence-based.

Cognitive Mapping

The central bar acts as an immediate landmark — guests understand at a glance where to order and where to sit. No confusion, no friction, faster table turns.

Acoustic Control

Perforated wood panels and living plant walls absorb kitchen machinery noise, guaranteeing a calm dining atmosphere despite high-output cooking operations running simultaneously.

ZoneLight LevelColour Temp.Purpose
Production Kitchens500–700 luxCool whiteSafety, alertness, precision
Dining Room2,700 KWarm whiteRelaxation, appetite stimulation
04 —

Equipment Overview

Pizza
  • Compact high-efficiency rotary stone oven
  • High-temperature deck (450°C+)
  • Dedicated cold dough station
Smash
  • Hard chrome flat-top griddle (easy clean)
  • Fresh-air induction hoods
  • High-speed bun toaster station
Japas / Poké
  • Dedicated raw preparation station
  • Refrigerated sushi display counter
  • Cold assembly line (Poké)
Shared
  • Rapid-cycle dishwasher (90s cycle)
  • Modular cold rooms (IoT-monitored)
  • Central POS system (4 brands, 1 terminal)

Food Safety & Operations

HACCP &
FLOOR PLANS

A fully automated, architecture-enforced food safety system — built into every square metre of The Hub's design.

00 —

Floor Plans & Circulation

The QUADRANT BCN floor plan is a masterpiece of operational engineering — strictly divided into functional zones to ensure a seamless Forward Flow (Marche en Avant). A clear physical barrier separates the Dirty Zone (loading dock, waste, raw storage) from the Clean Zone (preparation, assembly, guest dining).

Plan 01
General Layout
01 — General Layout
The Hybrid Ecosystem

The hybrid space balances high-volume production with a premium guest experience. Raw ingredients enter from the back and only move forward, exiting as finished products at the central pass. Every square foot optimised to meet the 40% public-facing requirement while maintaining a high-performance industrial core.

Plan 02
Guest Journey
02 — Guest Journey
Intuitive & Safe Circulation

Designed around Neuroarchitecture principles: Entrance → Digital Order → Shared Dining → Central Pick-up. The guest entrance is separated from the dedicated Rider's Portal. Guests remain strictly within the front-of-house safety zone, never intersecting with staff paths or raw food handling areas.

Plan 03
Cooks' Pathway
03 — Cooks' Pathway
Precision & Performance

Each of the four specialised kitchens functions as an independent high-performance quadrant. Staff enter through a dedicated technical access point, moving directly to changing rooms before their production zone. Movement follows Work Triangle ergonomics. The Forward Flow is strictly enforced: ingredients move from cold storage → preparation → heated/cooled pass.

Plan 04
Cashier & Runner Flow
04 — Cashier & Runner Flow
The Operational Pivot

Positioned at the central Bar Magnet, staff act as the bridge between the four production quadrants and the final customer. The runner's path is a high-efficiency circuit between the kitchen Pass and the Pick-up Window. Runners only handle final, packaged products — their path avoids the interior of cooking quadrants entirely.

01 —

Cold Storage & Temperature Control

Temperature management is the cornerstone of The Hub's HACCP system — preventing bacterial proliferation across all four concepts simultaneously.

Cold Chain
Separated Cold Rooms

Sectorised cold storage for raw materials: organic beef (Smash) is stored completely separately from raw fish (Japas / Poké) to prevent cross-contamination at the source.

Blast Chilling
Rapid Cooling Cells

Mandatory blast chillers bring prepared items — sauces, cooked dishes — from 63°C to 10°C in under two hours, meeting the strictest HACCP chilling protocols.

Monitoring
Calibrated Probe Thermometers

Core temperature checks at reception and throughout storage. IoT sensors on all shared refrigeration units provide continuous automated logging and instant alerts — zero manual gaps.

02 —

Preparation & Cooking Equipment

Each brand operates with its own dedicated equipment, ensuring operational independence and eliminating shared-surface contamination risks.

Pizza
Stone Oven & Dough Station

High-temperature pizza oven with a minimum 1.5 m capacity to accommodate the rectangular format. Paired with a professional dough mixer and a dedicated cold prep surface.

Smash
Chrome Griddle & Extraction

Professional flat-top griddle with a powerful extraction system and refrigerated drawers beneath the work surface to keep organic beef at optimal temperature until the moment of cooking.

Japas
Wok Burners & Steamers

High-power wok burners, steam cookers for gyozas and baos, and large-capacity rice cookers. Each piece of equipment is dedicated to this quadrant — never shared with adjacent concepts.

Poké
Refrigerated Prep Tables

Refrigerated saladette units maintain all fresh ingredients and toppings between 0°C and 4°C at all times during service — the cold chain is never broken, even during assembly.

03 —

Hygiene & Personnel Safety

In Spain, compliance with personnel hygiene and flow protocols is strictly enforced. The Hub is designed to exceed minimum requirements by architecture, not just procedure.

Handwashing
Non-Manual Handwash Stations

Foot-pedal or photoelectric-cell activated sinks, equipped with bactericidal soap dispensers and single-use paper towels — positioned at every kitchen entrance and prep zone.

Cross-contamination
Colour-Coded Cutting Boards

A strict colour-coding protocol prevents bacterial transfer between the four concepts.

Materials
Stainless Steel Throughout

All utensils and work surfaces are stainless steel — the only material authorised for professional kitchens due to its near-zero porosity and ease of sterilisation.

Red
Red meat
Blue
Raw fish
Green
Vegetables
Yellow
Cooked foods
04 —

Waste Management & Flow

Clear waste circuits are defined and physically segregated from food production flows — a non-negotiable requirement under Spanish health regulations.

Dishwashing
Dedicated Plonge · 85°C Rinse

The centralised dishwashing room is equipped with a professional short-cycle dishwasher. The final rinse cycle reaches 85°C — the HACCP-validated temperature for complete thermal disinfection.

Waste
Foot-Pedal Bins · Every Zone

Hermetically sealed, foot-operated waste bins are positioned in each kitchen zone. No manual lid contact — preventing hand-to-surface contamination during service.

Grease
Grease Trap System

A mandatory grease interceptor is installed before drainage — required by Barcelona municipal regulations, especially critical for the Smash (griddle) and Japas (deep fryer) concepts.

Legal & Compliance

LEGAL &
COMPLIANCE

QUADRANT BCN · Barcelona 22@ District

QUADRANT BCN is a hybrid gastronomic establishment operating under a "Restauració Mixta" licence — a model that distinguishes us from traditional industrial kitchens and anchors us in Barcelona's urban fabric with full regulatory compliance.

1. Business Identity & Licensing

QUADRANT BCN operates under a "Restauració Mixta" (Mixed Restoration) licence, in strict compliance with Barcelona's Urban Planning regulations (Pla d'Usos). We guarantee that a minimum of 40% of our total surface area is dedicated to public-facing use — including our guest dining area, the central bar, and the "Click & Collect" zone.

This hybrid model ensures our commitment to local urban harmony and the full transparency of our operations within the 22@ innovation district.

40%
Public-facing area
40+
Guest seats
4
Independent concepts

2. Food Safety & HACCP Standards

Our operational core is built on the highest sanitary standards, implementing a rigorous HACCP (Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points) system across all four specialised kitchens.

Forward Flow Principle

Raw materials enter through a dedicated rear entrance and only move forward through storage, preparation, and cooking — never reversing into a clean zone.

Path Segregation

"Clean" circuits (food preparation and service) never intersect with "dirty" circuits (waste disposal and raw material reception), eliminating cross-contamination by architecture.

Continuous Monitoring

All storage environments are equipped with IoT sensors providing 24/7 temperature control and automated HACCP logging across the full cold chain.

3. Operational Ergonomics & Well-being

Following the principles of Neuroarchitecture, our space is designed to optimise both staff performance and guest comfort.

Optimised Pathing

Dedicated pathways for Cooks, Runners, and Guests ensure fluid movement and reduce physical strain across all operational zones during peak service.

Sanitary Boundaries

Staff pathways are strictly separated from public areas, maintaining the highest hygiene standards without compromising the guest experience.

4. Allergen Transparency — EU Regulation 1169/2011

In accordance with EU Regulation 1169/2011, QUADRANT BCN provides full transparency regarding the 14 major allergens across all four concepts. Digital allergen matrices are available at every ordering kiosk and below.

Note: while each quadrant operates in a physically separated environment, they share a central logistical core. Customers with severe allergies should inform staff.

5. Data Privacy & GDPR

Your privacy is paramount. Any data collected through our "Click & Collect" platform is processed in strict accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). We use your information solely to facilitate your orders and improve your experience within THE HUB. You hold the right to access, rectify, and request deletion of your personal data at any time by contacting us at contact@thehub.com.
